item.title: Taiwanese Stocks Refresh Record High as AI Trade Stages Comeback
item.description: Taiwanese stocks rose to a new record as investors returned to the pre-Iran war trading theme of chasing AI shares amid hopes for easing tensions in the Middle East.

item.title: HSBC CEO Warns Mideast Conflict Is Denting Global Confidence
item.description: HSBC Holdings Plc Chief Executive Officer Georges Elhedery said the conflict in the Middle East and broader “uncertainties” are beginning to dent client confidence as investors navigate an increasingly volatile global landscape.

item.title: BlackRock’s Asia Private Credit Fund Sees China Borrower Default
item.description: A BlackRock Inc. private credit fund in Asia has suffered the first default by a borrower in its portfolio after a Chinese company failed to repay a loan, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Australia Consumer Confidence Plummets on Fuel Surge, Rate Hike
item.description: Australia’s consumer confidence tumbled in April as soaring fuel prices coupled with another interest-rate increase triggered the biggest fall since the pandemic.

item.title: Australia’s Sovereign Wealth Fund Reviews Some Investment Roles
item.description: Australia’s A$335 billion ($238 billion) sovereign wealth fund is reviewing 10 positions, including some investment roles, as part of a cost-cutting drive.

item.title: European Natural Gas Falls Amid Limited Trading in Asian Hours
item.description: European natural gas prices slipped in tepid early trading during Asian hours.

item.title: Trump Taps Pimco’s Erin Browne for Treasury International Affairs Job
item.description: President Donald Trump intends to nominate Erin Browne to serve as the Treasury Department’s under secretary for international affairs, a move that would fill a key role at the department, according to a statement Monday from Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent.

item.title: Colombia Credit Chief Eyes $4 Billion Buyback of External Bonds
item.description: Colombia is planning to repurchase some $4 billion of its bonds, according to public credit director Javier Cuellar.

item.title: Singapore Leads Asia in Tightening Policy on Oil-Price Shock
item.description: Singapore tightened its monetary policy settings in a widely expected move, becoming the first in Asia to respond to rising inflation risks stemming from a surge in energy prices linked to the Middle East conflict.

item.title: Antofagasta Signals Interest in Joining Argentina’s Copper Rush
item.description: Antofagasta Plc is taking an early look at opportunities in Argentina as sweeping policy changes revive interest in one of the world’s most promising new copper frontiers, Chief Executive Officer Iván Arriagada said in an interview.

item.title: RBA’s Hauser Doesn’t Have High Confidence Rates at Right Level
item.description: Australia’s central bank will need to set interest rates at a level that brings inflation back to the 2-3% target, Deputy Governor Andrew Hauser said, adding the board doesn’t have “high confidence” that they’re there yet.

item.title: Volatility Stymies Asian Carry Even as Returns Rise, ANZ Says
item.description: Elevated currency swings make carry trades unattractive in emerging Asia, according to Australia & New Zealand Banking Group, even with returns at the highest levels in nearly four years.

item.title: M&A Boom in Japan Is Fueling Record Corporate Bond Sales
item.description: Corporate bond issuance in Japan is poised to breach a record this year on growing funding demand for mergers and acquisitions as the economy continues to move away from three decades of deflation.

item.title: Fed’s Miran Sees Inflation Close to Target a Year From Now
item.description: Federal Reserve Governor Stephen Miran said the energy shock triggered by the Iran war has yet to impact longer-run inflation expectations, and he expects price pressures to return to the central bank’s target in a year’s time.

item.title: Blue Owl Private Credit Fund Raises $400 Million in Bond Market
item.description: A Blue Owl Capital Inc. private credit fund raised $400 million from bond investors Monday, marking the first deal of its kind in over a month, as worries about AI-disrupted software bets and looser lending standards have roiled the once red-hot industry.

item.title: UK’s Summer Gas Supply Looks Sufficient Despite War, Grid Says
item.description: Britain’s natural gas supply looks sufficient this summer despite the disruption to global energy flows caused by the Iran war, according to the grid manager.

item.title: Xi’s Petroyuan Dream Gets New Life From Trump’s War in Iran
item.description: The war in Iran is spurring a fresh wave of enthusiasm over prospects for China’s currency to more effectively rival the US dollar.

item.title: Singapore Interbank Rate Drops Toward Four-Year Low on Haven Bid
item.description: Singapore’s interbank rates are approaching four-year lows as haven demand due to the Iran war boosts inflows into the nation’s AAA rated assets.

item.title: Chinese Stocks’ Haven Appeal Tested by Tepid Earnings Outlook
item.description: The latest earnings from Chinese companies disappointed with more negative surprises, challenging the outlook for the country’s stocks after they enjoyed a haven-like status during the Iran war.

item.title: Texas Man Charged With Attempted Murder in Altman Attack
item.description: Local and federal prosecutors filed attempted murder and other criminal charges against the man arrested Friday for allegedly throwing a Molotov cocktail at the home of OpenAI Chief Executive Officer Sam Altman.

item.title: Stocks in Asia Poised to Climb on Iran Deal Hopes: Markets Wrap
item.description: Stocks in Asia were set to advance, tracking Wall Street gains as US President Donald Trump raised hopes for an Iran deal even as a blockade of the Strait of Hormuz took effect.

item.title: Gold Rises as Traders Weigh Potential to Revive US-Iran Talks
item.description: Gold edged higher after a two-day decline, as renewed optimism for a negotiated end to the US-Iran war eased concerns around inflation.

item.title: Raizen’s Creditors Said to Ask for 90% Equity Stake in Debt Swap
item.description: Raízen SA bondholders and bank creditors are asking for a stake of as much as 90% of the company in exchange for 45% of its debt in a restructuring, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Oracle Agrees to Buy Power From Bloom for AI Data Centers
item.description: Oracle Corp. agreed to purchase as much as 2.8 gigawatts of fuel-cell power from Bloom Energy Corp. to supply data centers for artificial intelligence work.

item.title: Oil Drops as US and Iran Weigh Meeting to Revive Ceasefire Talks
item.description: Oil fell as President Donald Trump said Tehran reached out over peace talks after the start of a US blockade of the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Microsoft Raises Surface Prices Sharply in Face of Memory Crunch
item.description: Microsoft Corp. raised prices sharply across its Surface-branded device lineup, becoming the latest personal computer maker to pass along costs fueled by a historic memory chip shortage.

item.title: New Zealand Dairy Farmers Pocket Billions From Huge Capital Payout
item.description: New Zealand dairy farmers are about to get a record multi-billion dollar, tax-free payout. They may need every cent of it.

item.title: Thoma Bravo Winds Down Growth Equity to Focus on Owning Firms
item.description: Thoma Bravo is winding down its growth equity business less than five years after the software-focused investing firm debuted it, according to people familiar with the matter. Instead, it’s focusing more on its core buyouts strategy, which owns controlling interests in established firms.

item.title: Kraken Crypto Exchange Says Criminal Group Claims Access to Some Customer Data
item.description: Kraken, one of the world’s oldest crypto exchanges, said it’s being extorted by a criminal group that claims to have access to some client account information.

item.title: USDA Wants Farmers to Help With Fertilizer Probe as Prices Soar
item.description: The Trump administration is seeking information from farmers to help with an ongoing Justice Department probe into high costs for fertilizer, machinery and other farm inputs.
